agent1_root_cause: Database connection pool exhaustion due to a suspected session leak

solutions.title: Fix session leak in rebalance_service.py (immediate code fix)
solutions.description: The logs show a session close was skipped at portfolio/rebalance_service.py:118 and that db connections are not returning to the pool after rebalance requests. Wrap every SessionLocal() usage in a `with` block (context manager) so the session is guaranteed to close on exit or exception.
steps: Replace bare `session = SessionLocal()` with `with SessionLocal() as session:`.
Verify all other SessionLocal call sites use context managers.
Deploy the fix (deployment version 2026.03.17-3 or hotfix branch).
solutions.risk: LOW — pure code change, no infra touch
cons: May not release existing leaked connections

solutions.title: Increase SQLAlchemy pool_size and max_overflow (short-term relief)
solutions.description: Raise pool_size from 20 to ~40 and max_overflow from 5 to 10 in SQLAlchemy config. This buys headroom while the code fix is prepared but does NOT fix the leak.
steps: Edit SQLAlchemy engine creation: `create_engine(..., pool_size=40, max_overflow=10, pool_timeout=30, pool_recycle=1800)`
Monitor pool metrics; confirm checked_out stabilises.
solutions.risk: MEDIUM — higher pool size increases load on Postgres; verify max_connections headroom first
cons: Masks the leak rather than fixing it
Postgres max_connections may be a hard ceiling
Will exhaust again if leak rate is high

solutions.title: Temporarily disable the rebalance endpoint / rate-limit it
solutions.description: Return 503 or queue rebalance requests while the fix is deployed. This stops new connections from being leaked while the root cause is addressed.
steps: Add an Nginx location block to return 503 for POST /api/v1/orders/rebalance.
Or set a feature flag in app config to short-circuit the rebalance handler.
Communicate maintenance window to affected users.
solutions.risk: LOW infra risk, HIGH business impact — users cannot rebalance

solutions.title: Increase Postgres max_connections and tune superuser reservation
solutions.description: Postgres rejected connections with 'remaining connection slots are reserved for superuser'. Raising max_connections and reducing superuser_reserved_connections gives the app more slots, but only treats the symptom.
steps: Check current value: `SHOW max_connections;` in psql.
Edit postgresql.conf: `max_connections = 200` (or appropriate value for RAM).
Also consider: `superuser_reserved_connections = 3` (default is 3, can lower to 2).
Reload Postgres: `pg_ctlcluster <ver> main reload` or `systemctl reload postgresql`.
solutions.risk: MEDIUM — each Postgres connection uses ~5-10 MB RAM; validate server memory first
cons: Memory risk if connections accumulate indefinitely

solutions.title: Rollback deployment 2026.03.17-2
solutions.description: Logs explicitly note that deployment 2026.03.17-2 (deployed at 11:34) touched the db session lifecycle in the rebalance workflow, and the incident started at 11:40. Rolling back to 2026.03.17-1 would immediately stop new leaks.
steps: Trigger rollback via CI/CD or manually swap the application bundle.
Restart gunicorn: `systemctl restart gunicorn` or equivalent.
Monitor pool metrics to confirm connections return.
pros: Fastest way to stop the leak
Strong causal evidence in logs supports this
cons: Need to identify what changed in that deploy

risky_actions_avoid.title: Kill all idle Postgres connections manually
risky_actions_avoid.reason: pg_terminate_backend on active sessions can corrupt in-flight transactions.

risky_actions_avoid.title: Set pool_timeout=0 to disable waiting
risky_actions_avoid.reason: Would cause immediate errors for all requests instead of slow degradation.

notes: S5 (rollback) stops the bleeding fastest. S1 (code fix) is the permanent cure. S2 and S4 are mitigations that should follow S1 if pool settings need tuning post-fix. S3 is a circuit-breaker of last resort.

handoff_for_agent3.confirmed_root_cause: Database connection pool exhaustion due to a suspected session leak
